issue.title: About Page Edits
issue.body:
- [ ] Reorganize layout of the site
- [ ] Improve CSS for entire page
- [ ] Get contact information from co-owners
- [ ] Add testimonials and populate dynamically
- [ ] Get a couple founding stories from co-owners

comment.body:
## Order of the about page

Heidi said the layout they wanted for the about page is

1. Company Summary
2. Company Stories (funny founding stories)
3. Testimonials
4. Founder Contact and Bio

We can add more pictures throughout

## Testimonials

*Make sure these use correct grammer

```text
The thrill of race day brings so much adrenaline. I beat my 5k time by 2 whole minutes!
- Alex Dombroski

I really felt I achieved my goal and loved the feeling of accomplishment. 
- Zack Dinger

It’s so cool meeting so many people who all love running at Run club 
- Nathan Nielson 

It’s a great and fun opportunity to get the blood moving!
- Mitch Motzkus

The downhill is the best part!
- Taylor Hancey

The T-shirts, Medals, and music are so hype!!!
- Bree

Grateful for all the people and volunteers!
- Livi

People who run are so cool. 
- Evelyn

The event is so high energy and fun!
- Addi

My favorite part was the halfway point in middle of nowhere and seeing my running buddy. 
- Luke Darracq

The sunshine and scenery were beautiful!
- Olivia Alexander

I was going to run the 5k, but then I ran the half marathon instead!
- Parker Kaufman 
```

review.body:
## Pull request overview Fixes the FAQ page rendering by aligning `faq.js` with the `questions.json` schema: FAQ items use the `tag` field (not `t`) to build the per-question CSS class. **Changes:** - Updated FAQ item template to use `item.tag` when building the `
` class name. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

issue.title: Member Page Overhaul
issue.body:
Leverage Astro's SSG capabilities to create a regularly updating webpage displaying members of the society.

## Planning

- [ ] Research the Github API to see how we can list members of an organization, and sort them by activity or join date
- [ ] Plan for hard-coded constants that can be easily replaced to show new society leadership
- [ ] Ensure endpoints used work in dev environment even if unauthenticated
- [ ] Create a design for the page.

## Setup

- [ ] Create an organization-level Github App with fine-grained permissions to get member information
- [ ] Modify the deploy pipeline so it has access to the Github App tokens
- [ ] Modify the deploy pipeline so it redeploys once a week
- [ ] Install [octokit](https://github.com/octokit) or other helpful libraries for interacting with Github API on node.js in the ssg (optional)


> [!NOTE]
> All of this should be done within SSG, so the Github App keys/secrets don't get deployed

## Application

- [ ] Display data on the page for each member
- [ ] Style the design
- [ ] add steps as necessary

comment.body:
## Goal
Way to see members of the society and their information using GitHub API..

issue.title: Member Page Overhaul
issue.body:
Leverage Astro's SSG capabilities to create a regularly updating webpage displaying members of the society.

## Planning

- [ ] Research the Github API to see how we can list members of an organization, and sort them by activity or join date
- [ ] Plan for hard-coded constants that can be easily replaced to show new society leadership
- [ ] Ensure endpoints used work in dev environment even if unauthenticated
- [ ] Create a design for the page.

## Setup

- [ ] Create an organization-level Github App with fine-grained permissions to get member information
- [ ] Modify the deploy pipeline so it has access to the Github App tokens
- [ ] Modify the deploy pipeline so it redeploys once a week
- [ ] Install [octokit](https://github.com/octokit) or other helpful libraries for interacting with Github API on node.js in the ssg (optional)


> [!NOTE]
> All of this should be done within SSG, so the Github App keys/secrets don't get deployed

## Application

- [ ] Display data on the page for each member
- [ ] Style the design
- [ ] add steps as necessary
